passages.title: Cloze Test 17-21
passages.text: The manager postponed the meeting because some board members were unable to attend due to a flight delay.

passages.title: Cloze Test 22-26
passages.text:
Student: Professor, could you explain the significance of the Enlightenment? 
 Professor: Certainly. It was a movement that emphasized reason and individualism over tradition. 
 Student: ______ 
 Professor: Precisely. It laid the groundwork for modern democratic ideals and scientific inquiry.

questions.question: The sudden ______ of the pandemic forced many small businesses to declare bankruptcy within months.

options.A: advent
options.B: diminution
options.C: mitigation
options.D: validation
options.E: rectification

questions.question: Researchers are attempting to determine if the new drug has any ______ effects on long-term cognitive function.

options.A: superficial
options.B: detrimental
options.C: negligible
options.D: benevolent
options.E: fleeting

questions.question: The diplomat acted ______ during the negotiations, carefully balancing the interests of both nations to avoid conflict.

options.A: abruptly
options.B: judiciously
options.C: haphazardly
options.D: scarcely
options.E: mutually

questions.question: While some argue that globalization leads to cultural homogenization, ______.

options.A: it has also facilitated the preservation of indigenous languages through digital platforms
options.B: consequently, most people prefer local products over imported ones
options.C: it is because the economic benefits are distributed equally
options.D: therefore, local cultures are completely eradicated by Western influence
options.E: since the process is inevitable in the modern technological era

questions.question: ______ unless immediate measures are taken to reduce carbon emissions globally.

options.A: The rise in sea levels will become irreversible
options.B: Renewable energy sources are becoming more affordable
options.C: Many countries have already signed the Paris Agreement
options.D: Environmentalists are hopeful about the future of the planet
options.E: Climate change is a complex phenomenon with many variables

questions.question: Despite the advancements in artificial intelligence, ______.

options.A: computers are now able to process data faster than humans
options.B: the technology is being integrated into every aspect of life
options.C: it still lacks the nuance of human emotional intelligence
options.D: many jobs are being automated to increase efficiency
options.E: the cost of hardware has decreased significantly

questions.question: Archaeologists recently discovered a hidden chamber in the pyramid, ______.

options.A: which suggests that there are still many secrets to be uncovered
options.B: although the pyramid was built thousands of years ago
options.C: so that they could preserve the artifacts inside
options.D: because they were using ground-penetrating radar
options.E: it was filled with gold and ancient manuscripts

questions.question: Cloze Test (1/5): The Industrial Revolution was a period of (1)______ social and economic change.

options.A: profound
options.B: trivial
options.C: stable
options.D: ancient
options.E: redundant

questions.question: Cloze Test (2/5): It transitioned societies from agrarian economies (2)______ industrial ones.

options.A: to
options.B: by
options.C: from
options.D: at
options.E: with

questions.question: Cloze Test (3/5): (3)______, this shift led to significant urban growth as workers moved to cities.

options.A: However
options.B: Consequently
options.C: Otherwise
options.D: Instead
options.E: Initially

questions.question: Cloze Test (4/5): Living conditions in these cities were often poor, (4)______ to the spread of diseases.

options.A: leading
options.B: leads
options.C: lead
options.D: led
options.E: having led

questions.question: Cloze Test (5/5): Today, historians (5)______ the long-term impact of this era on modern capitalism.

options.A: are still analyzing
options.B: had analyzed
options.C: will analyze
options.D: were analyzing
options.E: analyzed

questions.question: Modern tıp, genetik araştırmalar sayesinde birçok kalıtsal hastalığın tedavisinde önemli ilerlemeler kaydetmiştir.

options.A: Modern medicine has made significant progress in treating many hereditary diseases thanks to genetic research.
options.B: Genetic research is the reason why modern medicine can treat hereditary diseases.
options.C: Significant progress in modern medicine depends on genetic research for treating diseases.
options.D: Hereditary diseases are being treated by modern medicine through genetic studies.
options.E: Thanks to genetic research, many diseases are now being treated by modern medicine.

questions.question: Eğitimin temel amacı, bireylere sadece bilgi aktarmak değil, aynı zamanda eleştirel düşünme becerileri kazandırmaktır.

options.A: The primary goal of education is not only to transfer knowledge to individuals but also to provide them with critical thinking skills.
options.B: Transferring knowledge and critical thinking are the two main aims of the education system.
options.C: Education aims to give individuals critical thinking skills rather than just transferring information.
options.D: Not only knowledge but also critical thinking skills should be the aim of education.
options.E: Individuals need both knowledge and critical thinking skills, which is the aim of education.

questions.question: Psychologists suggest that social media usage significantly affects the mental health of adolescents.

options.A: Psikologlar, sosyal medya kullanımının ergenlerin ruh sağlığını önemli ölçüde etkilediğini öne sürmektedir.
options.B: Ergenlerin ruh sağlığı, psikologlara göre sosyal medya kullanımından etkilenmektedir.
options.C: Sosyal medya kullanımı, psikologların dediği gibi ergenlerin zihnini etkiler.
options.D: Psikologlar, ergenlerin ruh sağlığı üzerinde sosyal medyanın etkili olduğunu düşünürler.
options.E: Sosyal medyanın ergen ruh sağlığı üzerindeki etkisi psikologlarca araştırılmaktadır.

questions.question: Economic growth is often measured by the increase in a country's gross domestic product (GDP).

options.A: Ekonomik büyüme genellikle bir ülkenin gayri safi yurt içi hasılasındaki (GSYİH) artışla ölçülür.
options.B: GSYİH artışı, bir ülkenin ekonomik büyümesini ölçmenin tek yoludur.
options.C: Ekonomik büyüme, bir ülkenin GSYİH'si arttığında gerçekleşen bir durumdur.
options.D: Bir ülkenin GSYİH'si artarsa, o ülkenin ekonomisi büyümüş sayılır.
options.E: Genellikle ekonomik büyüme, ülkenin GSYİH'sine bakılarak hesaplanır.

questions.question: Boşluk 17 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Because of a flight delay, some board members could not come, so the manager put off the meeting.
options.B: The meeting was held despite the fact that some board members were late because of their flight.
options.C: The manager decided to cancel the meeting since most of the board members were stuck at the airport.
options.D: The flight delay was the reason why the manager had to attend the meeting alone.
options.E: If the flight had not been delayed, the manager would have started the meeting on time.

questions.question: Boşluk 18 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: This scientific discovery is one of the few that has instantly and deeply changed how we perceive the universe.
options.B: Scientific discoveries usually take a long time to change our understanding of the universe.
options.C: Our understanding of the universe is rarely impacted by scientific discoveries.
options.D: No other discovery has ever influenced our understanding of the universe as much as this one.
options.E: The impact of this discovery on the universe was neither immediate nor profound.

questions.question: Boşluk 19 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: The hikers survived the blizzard only because the rescue team intervened just in time.
options.B: The rescue team arrived too late to save the hikers from the blizzard.
options.C: The hikers managed to survive the blizzard without any help from the rescue team.
options.D: Had the rescue team intervened earlier, the hikers would have been safer.
options.E: Even with the rescue team's intervention, the hikers barely survived the blizzard.

questions.question: Boşluk 20 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Even though she had worked in the field for a long time, she struggled to learn how to use the new software.
options.B: Her experience in the field helped her master the new software very quickly.
options.C: She found the software difficult because she lacked experience in that specific field.
options.D: The new software was so easy that her extensive experience was not even necessary.
options.E: Because she was experienced, she expected the software to be much easier than it was.

questions.question: Boşluk 21 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: I think it's mostly due to the lack of vegetation and the abundance of concrete.
options.B: But rural areas are also experiencing record-breaking heatwaves.
options.C: Do you think the report is accurate about the data collection?
options.D: I wonder if air conditioning contributes to this phenomenon.
options.E: We should move to the countryside to escape the heat.

questions.question: Boşluk 22 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: So, it basically challenged the absolute authority of the monarchy and the church?
options.B: Was it mainly focused on artistic expression during the Renaissance?
options.C: Did it have any impact on the Industrial Revolution later on?
options.D: I thought it was just about the discovery of new trade routes.
options.E: Why did it take so long for these ideas to spread across Europe?

questions.question: Boşluk 23 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: I might just start with a small amount to see how it goes.
options.B: I'm sure the prices will only go up from now on.
options.C: Is there any way to guarantee a profit in this market?
options.D: I'll put all my savings into Bitcoin tomorrow.
options.E: What do you think about traditional stock markets instead?

questions.question: Boşluk 24 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Should I undergo some tests to find the underlying cause?
options.B: I think I just need more caffeine in the morning.
options.C: Can you prescribe me some sleeping pills?
options.D: Is it possible that I am just working too hard?
options.E: Will these symptoms go away on their own if I rest?

questions.question: Boşluk 25 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: It encourages the reuse, repair, and recycling of materials to extend their lifecycle.
options.B: However, many industries are still reluctant to change their production methods.
options.C: The linear model is more profitable for short-term financial gains.
options.D: Many consumers are unaware of the benefits of sustainable products.
options.E: Waste management has become a global crisis in the 21st century.

questions.question: Boşluk 26 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: However, the brain is an interconnected network where no part acts entirely alone.
options.B: Fear is one of the most basic survival instincts in all mammals.
options.C: Many psychological disorders are linked to malfunctions in the amygdala.
options.D: Technological advances like fMRI have made these discoveries possible.
options.E: The brain remains one of the most mysterious organs in the human body.

questions.question: Renewable energy is often criticized for its intermittency, as solar and wind power depend on weather conditions. ______. Advancements in battery technology now allow us to store excess energy generated during sunny or windy days for use when the sun isn't shining or the wind isn't blowing.

options.A: To address this, researchers are developing more efficient energy storage systems.
options.B: This means that fossil fuels will always be necessary as a backup.
options.C: Governments are hesitant to invest in such unreliable energy sources.
options.D: The cost of installing solar panels is still too high for many households.
options.E: Energy consumption is at an all-time high due to industrialization.

questions.question: In literature, the 'anti-hero' is a protagonist who lacks traditional heroic qualities such as idealism and morality. ______. Instead of being purely good, they are often deeply flawed, making them more relatable to the audience. This complexity allows authors to explore the darker aspects of human nature.

options.A: They may act out of self-interest or exhibit questionable behavior.
options.B: The hero always triumphs over evil in the end of the story.
options.C: Modern readers prefer characters who are perfect and inspiring.
options.D: This literary device was first introduced during the Romantic era.
options.E: Most villains in classic novels are also considered anti-heroes.

questions.question: It is not until you travel abroad that you truly appreciate the comforts of your own home.

options.A: You only realize how comfortable your home is after you have traveled to other countries.
options.B: Traveling abroad is the only way to make your home feel more comfortable.
options.C: If you don't travel, you will never be happy with your living conditions.
options.D: The comforts of home are superior to anything you can find in foreign countries.
options.E: Traveling abroad helps you find more comfortable places to live than your home.

questions.question: Hardly anyone expected the underdog team to win the championship against the defending giants.

options.A: Almost no one thought the less powerful team would beat the previous champions.
options.B: Everyone was certain that the underdog team would lose the final match.
options.C: The defending champions were expected to win by a very large margin.
options.D: It was a surprise to no one when the underdog team finally won.
options.E: The championship was cancelled because the defending giants were too strong.

questions.question: Should you require any further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact our customer service department.

options.A: Feel free to get in touch with our customer service if you need more help.
options.B: You must contact customer service immediately if you have any problems.
options.C: Our customer service is available only for those who require technical help.
options.D: Do not contact us unless it is absolutely necessary for your assistance.
options.E: If you are hesitant, our customer service department can help you.

questions.question: (I) The Great Wall of China is one of the most impressive architectural feats in history. (II) It was built over several centuries to protect the Chinese Empire from invasions. (III) The wall stretches over 13,000 miles across various terrains. (IV) Tourism in China has seen a significant increase in the last decade. (V) Its construction involved millions of workers and massive amounts of stone and brick.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) Quantum mechanics is a fundamental theory in physics that describes the physical properties of nature at the scale of atoms. (II) It differs significantly from classical physics, which focuses on macroscopic objects. (III) Many students find physics to be one of the most difficult subjects in university. (IV) One of its key principles is the wave-particle duality of energy and matter. (V) This theory has led to the development of technologies like transistors and lasers.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants use sunlight to synthesize nutrients. (II) This process involves the chlorophyll in the leaves absorbing carbon dioxide and water. (III) Oxygen is released as a byproduct, which is vital for the survival of most life forms. (IV) Some plants have adapted to grow in extreme environments like deserts. (V) Without photosynthesis, the Earth's atmosphere would lack the oxygen necessary for respiration.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: Passage: The Renaissance was a fervent period of European cultural, artistic, political and economic ‘rebirth’ following the Middle Ages. Generally described as taking place from the 14th century to the 17th century, the Renaissance promoted the rediscovery of classical philosophy, literature and art. Some of the greatest thinkers, authors, statesmen, scientists and artists in human history thrived during this era, while global exploration opened up new lands and cultures to European commerce. Question: According to the passage, what was a primary characteristic of the Renaissance?

options.A: The rejection of classical Greek and Roman philosophies.
options.B: A total decline in economic activity across the European continent.
options.C: The rediscovery and promotion of classical art and literature.
options.D: The isolation of European cultures from the rest of the world.
options.E: A period where scientific inquiry was strictly forbidden.

questions.question: Passage: The Renaissance was a fervent period of European cultural, artistic, political and economic ‘rebirth’ following the Middle Ages. Generally described as taking place from the 14th century to the 17th century, the Renaissance promoted the rediscovery of classical philosophy, literature and art. Some of the greatest thinkers, authors, statesmen, scientists and artists in human history thrived during this era, while global exploration opened up new lands and cultures to European commerce. Question: What does the passage suggest about the impact of global exploration during the Renaissance?

options.A: It led to the downfall of European economic systems.
options.B: It introduced new lands and cultures to European trade.
options.C: It was the main cause of the Middle Ages.
options.D: It discouraged scientists from pursuing new discoveries.
options.E: It focused primarily on internal European politics.

questions.question: Passage: The Renaissance was a fervent period of European cultural, artistic, political and economic ‘rebirth’ following the Middle Ages. Generally described as taking place from the 14th century to the 17th century, the Renaissance promoted the rediscovery of classical philosophy, literature and art. Some of the greatest thinkers, authors, statesmen, scientists and artists in human history thrived during this era, while global exploration opened up new lands and cultures to European commerce. Question: Based on the passage, the Renaissance can be described as ______.

options.A: A transition from the Middle Ages to a period of cultural renewal.
options.B: A brief 50-year interval of artistic experimentation.
options.C: An era focused solely on the development of military technology.
options.D: A time when philosophical thought was at its lowest point.
options.E: The period that preceded the Middle Ages.

questions.question: Passage: Artificial Intelligence (AI) refers to the simulation of human intelligence in machines that are programmed to think like humans and mimic their actions. The term may also be applied to any machine that exhibits traits associated with a human mind such as learning and problem-solving. As technology advances, previous benchmarks that defined AI become outdated. For example, machines that calculate basic functions are no longer considered artificial intelligence, since this function is now expected as a standard computer capability. Question: According to the passage, how is the definition of AI changing over time?

options.A: It is becoming more restricted to only human-looking robots.
options.B: Functions that were once considered AI are now seen as standard.
options.C: It is no longer associated with learning or problem-solving.
options.D: It is becoming less relevant as technology fails to advance.
options.E: The definition has remained identical since its inception.

questions.question: Passage: Artificial Intelligence (AI) refers to the simulation of human intelligence in machines that are programmed to think like humans and mimic their actions. The term may also be applied to any machine that exhibits traits associated with a human mind such as learning and problem-solving. As technology advances, previous benchmarks that defined AI become outdated. For example, machines that calculate basic functions are no longer considered artificial intelligence, since this function is now expected as a standard computer capability. Question: Which of the following is a trait associated with the human mind that AI tries to mimic?

options.A: Physical strength and speed.
options.B: Emotional volatility and irrationality.
options.C: Learning and problem-solving.
options.D: Biological growth and aging.
options.E: The ability to consume nutrients.

questions.question: Passage: Artificial Intelligence (AI) refers to the simulation of human intelligence in machines that are programmed to think like humans and mimic their actions. The term may also be applied to any machine that exhibits traits associated with a human mind such as learning and problem-solving. As technology advances, previous benchmarks that defined AI become outdated. For example, machines that calculate basic functions are no longer considered artificial intelligence, since this function is now expected as a standard computer capability. Question: What is the main idea of the passage?

options.A: AI will eventually replace human intelligence entirely.
options.B: The definition and benchmarks of AI evolve alongside technological progress.
options.C: Calculating basic functions is the most advanced form of AI.
options.D: Machines are unable to mimic human learning processes.
options.E: AI is a static field that does not require further research.
